description: Defines RBAC permissions per role. Used by the tool boundary guard to enforce read/write access before any tool executes. Permissions follow the format action:scope where scope is 'all_tickets' (any tenant-local ticket), 'own_tickets' (only tickets reported by or assigned to the requesting user), or a specific resource type.

roles.description: Read-only access to tickets the user is directly involved in (reporter or assignee). Cannot view other users' tickets.
End user who can view and create their own tickets. Cannot view tickets belonging to other users.
Frontline support agent. Can read all tickets within the tenant, update work notes, and create tickets on behalf of users.
Specialist agent. Can read all tickets and update work notes within the tenant.
Specialist agent. Can read all tickets and update work notes within the tenant.
Application support agent. Can read all tickets and update work notes within the tenant.
Manages change requests. Can read all tickets and approve or reject changes.
Manages problem records. Can read all tickets and manage problem lifecycle.
Security specialist. Can read all tickets and update work notes.
Cloud infrastructure specialist. Can read all tickets and update work notes.
Manages configuration items. Can read all tickets and manage CMDB records.
Manages IT assets. Can read all tickets and manage asset records.
Manages knowledge base articles. Can read all tickets and publish KB articles.
Senior leadership. Full read access across all tickets and resources within the tenant.
Team or department manager. Read access across all tickets within the tenant for oversight; cannot create or modify tickets.
Standard end user. Can read tickets they reported or are assigned to; out of audience for cross-tenant summarisation. Exercises the authz-recheck deny path on UC-1.
